notes: Massive mechanical regolith processing plants purpose-built for water extraction at dry sites (0–15% water ice).
Intake hoppers collect bulk regolith; counter-rotating centrifugal drums separate fine ice grains from silicate waste.
Low-temperature bake-out chambers (~400 K) sublimate trapped volatiles; cryocondensers recover water.
Output is a flat rated water production regardless of ice concentration — ice fraction gates deployment only.
Very heavy equipment (10–270 t) but modest power draw — most work is mechanical and low-grade thermal.
Multiple units at the same facility sum their extraction rates.
Suited for lunar highlands, Mercury equatorial sites, and other low-ice locations where Heat Drill systems cannot operate.
